releases.title: collectZ v3.10.9
releases.summary: This patch keeps the mobile header cleanup moving by reducing secondary filter bulk on dense utility pages. Wishlist and Capture Inbox now keep search and primary actions immediately visible while putting secondary filters behind a compact mobile readback control.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared mobile secondary-filter disclosure primitive.
Moved Wishlist type filtering behind a compact mobile readback while keeping desktop controls visible.
Moved Capture Inbox type, source, and review filters behind a compact mobile readback while keeping status primary.
Preserved live search behavior and removed redundant mobile Search-button chrome from these utility headers.
Added a backlog entry for the remaining header/search refinement work discovered during the UI/UX pass.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.8
releases.summary: This patch brings utility pages into the mobile header cleanup line. Wishlist, Import, Integrations, and Capture Inbox now use a shared utility page header pattern with their main search, filter, or section controls kept in the fixed page header and compacted after the user scrolls into the page content.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared `UtilityPageHeader` primitive for utility-page title, subtitle, actions, and workflow-specific controls.
Extended `FixedPageShell` so utility pages can react to body scroll and compact their fixed header.
Moved Wishlist status/search filters into the fixed header and compacted the add action on scroll.
Moved Import source tabs into the fixed header so the source selector stays available while the import form scrolls.
Moved the mobile Integrations selector into the fixed header while keeping the desktop integration tabs in the page body.
Converted Capture Inbox to the fixed shell and moved capture status, source, search, and review filters into the shared utility header.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.7
releases.summary: This patch starts the shared page-header/search-toolbar path for the mobile interface cleanup line. The main library-style pages now use one toolbar primitive, and the toolbar compacts after the user scrolls into the content.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared `PageHeaderSearchToolbar` primitive for page title, count, search, filter controls, view toggle, sort, and add action.
Migrated media Library, Art, Collectibles, Events, and Loans onto the shared toolbar path.
Added scroll-aware compaction for media Library, Art, Collectibles, and Events so the toolbar gives back vertical space while browsing.
Kept page-specific filters local to each surface through toolbar slots instead of hiding business rules inside the primitive.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.6
releases.summary: This patch clarifies page-title ownership across the library surfaces: desktop library pages now name the section being viewed, while mobile keeps location in the compact app header instead of repeating it in the page toolbar.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Changed desktop media library headings from generic `Library` to the active section name, such as `Movies`, `TV`, `Books`, `Audio`, `Games`, or `Comics`.
Kept the aggregate all-library review route labeled `Library`.
Hid duplicate page-title/count groups from mobile library, Art, Collectibles, and Events toolbars because the mobile app shell already shows the active page title.
Preserved mobile search, filter, view-toggle, sort, and add controls in their existing page toolbars.
Updated browser regression coverage for mobile shell title ownership and the new desktop media-library heading.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.5
releases.summary: This patch replaces the mobile brand-style app header with a compact utility header that keeps navigation accessible while giving the space back to the active page.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Replaced the mobile hamburger button with the collectZ mark as the navigation trigger.
Kept the navigation trigger accessible with the same open-navigation behavior and expanded-state readback.
Removed the mobile `COLLECTZ` wordmark and role/workspace readback from the app header.
Added active page title readback in the mobile app header, including Dashboard, Loans, Wishlist, Import, and Integrations.
Updated the sidebar wordmark casing from `COLLECTZ` to `collectZ`.
Extended mobile browser regression coverage for the compact app header and nav trigger.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.4
releases.summary: This patch brings Loans onto the fixed-header page pattern added for the other utility pages in `3.10.3`.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Converted Loans to use the shared `FixedPageShell` primitive.
Kept the Loans title, search, status filters, and summary counters stationary while loan rows scroll underneath.
Extended the mobile utility-page browser regression so Loans is covered with Wishlist, Import, and Admin > Integrations.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.3
releases.summary: This patch brings Wishlist, Import, and Admin > Integrations onto the same fixed-header page pattern used by the library surfaces.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Added a shared `FixedPageShell` primitive for pages with stationary headers and independently scrolling content.
Converted Wishlist to keep its title/add-item header fixed while wishlist search, filters, and rows scroll underneath.
Converted Import to keep its page title and active-library readback fixed while source tabs and import controls scroll underneath.
Converted Admin > Integrations to keep its page title fixed while integration settings scroll underneath.
Added browser regression coverage for the three exception pages so page-level scrolling cannot carry their headers away again.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.2
releases.summary: This patch stabilizes the mobile app header so the CollectZ/menu bar stays in place while library content scrolls underneath it.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Changed the dashboard shell to use dynamic viewport height on mobile-oriented browsers.
Made the mobile app header sticky with a stable z-index.
Preserved the existing mobile header visuals, navigation behavior, and library page toolbars.
Added browser regression coverage that scrolls mobile library pages and verifies the app header does not move or create horizontal overflow.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.1
releases.summary: This patch tightens the mobile search and action areas across the main library surfaces so Library, Collectibles, Art, and Events feel more usable on phone-width screens.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Made mobile library toolbars more compact without introducing a new visual pattern.
Let Library search, quick filters, view toggles, sort, and add actions share predictable mobile rows.
Collapsed mobile title/action rows so view mode, sort, and add no longer consume a separate toolbar band.
Paired search with quick filters where possible, and kept bulk select controls out of the mobile header chrome.
Hid long library description copy on phone-width Collectibles, Art, and Events headers so controls start sooner.
Kept Events date filters visible while tightening their row layout.
Added a browser regression that checks mobile Library, Collectibles, and Events toolbars do not overflow and stay within expected compact heights.

releases.title: collectZ v3.10.0
releases.summary: This release starts the `3.10` usability and collection-maintenance line. The first slice keeps the Dashboard contained on mobile so recent syncs, recent activity, failure details, and panel content do not force horizontal page overflow.

details.heading: Highlights
bullets: Established `3.10` as a focused usability and collection-maintenance release line.
Hardened Dashboard panels, metric buttons, recent sync rows, recent activity rows, and long failed-sync messages against mobile horizontal overflow.
Renamed the Dashboard attention surface to `Review` and added compact mobile section tabs so Review categories stay readable on narrow screens.
Added exact row-level clues for missing-identifier Review items so users can see what kind of identifier would improve matching accuracy.
Added a mobile Dashboard browser regression that checks the page does not exceed the viewport width.
Kept desktop Dashboard layout behavior intact.
